patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH 1/2] mm: remove CONFIG_ARCH_ENABLE_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE
patches_acked.patch_summary: This patch removes the CONFIG_ARCH_ENABLE_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE configuration option, which enables or disables memory hot removal functionality in the Linux kernel.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged that HOTREMOVE has been a long-standing issue and appreciated the cleanup, but no specific technical details were provided to address any potential issues or concerns.
review_comments.raw_body:
HOTREMOVE has long been a thorn in my side, I appreciate you cleaning
this up.

Reviewed-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Lorenzo (Oracle)
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Lorenzo made a lighthearted comment about Gregory's patch, but did not provide any technical feedback or suggestions.

Reviewer noted that the patch description does not mention any specific issues or problems with CONFIG_ARCH_ENABLE_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE, and requested more context on why it's being removed.
review_comments.raw_body:
That is quite the HOTTAKE young Gregory!

(It seems my pun crimes are continuing)

---

Cheers, Lorenzo

_______________________________________________
linux-riscv mailing list
linux-riscv@lists.infradead.org
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-riscv

review_comments.author: David (Arm)
review_comments.summary: Reviewer questioned the necessity of a separate MEMORY_HOTREMOVE config option, noting that it's only used in conjunction with MIGRATION and some scattered CONFIG_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE instances, and suggested removing it.
review_comments.raw_body:
On a long cardrive today I was wondering: do we still need a separate
MEMORY_HOTREMOVE

Now we'll have

config MEMORY_HOTREMOVE
	bool "Allow for memory hot remove"
	select HAVE_BOOTMEM_INFO_NODE if (X86_64 || PPC64)
	depends on MEMORY_HOTPLUG
	select MIGRATION

I'll try to get rid of that HAVE_BOOTMEM_INFO_NODE shite next.

Then it's really just MIGRATION and some sprinkled
CONFIG_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE. Not a lot of code.

-- 
Cheers,

David

_______________________________________________
linux-riscv mailing list
linux-riscv@lists.infradead.org
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-riscv

review_comments.author: Zi Yan
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Zi Yan questioned the purpose of removing CONFIG_ARCH_ENABLE_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE, wondering if it's being merged into HOTPLUG instead and inquiring about the reason for only keeping HOTPLUG without HOTREMOVE.

Reviewer Zi Yan pointed out that the patch description does not mention any RISC-V specific issues or considerations, and requested that the patch be revised to address these concerns.
review_comments.raw_body:
You mean merge HOTREMOVE into HOTPLUG? I wondered the same. Is there
a reason to only have HOTPLUG without HOTREMOVE?

---

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

_______________________________________________
linux-riscv mailing list
linux-riscv@lists.infradead.org
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-riscv

review_comments.author: David (Arm)
review_comments.summary: Reviewer David (Arm) expressed uncertainty about removing CONFIG_ARCH_ENABLE_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE, citing that some architectures like s390x did not support HOTREMOVE in the past and questioned whether anyone would want to build a kernel with HOTPLUG but without the overhead for unplug+migration.
review_comments.raw_body:
In the past some architectures (e.g., s390x) did not support HOTREMOVE.
Then it made sense. I'm not sure if someone would want to build a kernel
with HOTPLUG but not have the bit of overhead for unplug+migration.

So I would guess nothing major speaks against it.

-- 
Cheers,

David

_______________________________________________

http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-riscv

review_comments.author: Zi Yan
review_comments.summary: Reviewer found the patch reasonable and did not raise any specific technical concerns.
review_comments.raw_body:
Sounds reasonable to me.

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi
